executive_summary: This session shows strong room presence and engagement. Focus on refining gesture size to match emphasis and softening facial tone for warmer delivery. Also, be mindful of turning back to the audience sooner after board checks and maintaining an open stance.

top_strengths.title: Alert room presence
top_strengths.evidence: 00:00-00:15 kept the head and eyes visibly engaged with the room. 00:15-00:30 kept the head and eyes visibly engaged with the room.
top_strengths.what_to_repeat: Keep the same quick room checks that make the lecture feel attentive.

top_strengths.title: Open-palm explanatory delivery
top_strengths.evidence: 00:15-00:30 included open-palm explanatory gestures that supported the explanation.
top_strengths.what_to_repeat: Keep the same open-palm gesture shape when emphasizing key ideas.

top_strengths.title: Distributed room engagement
top_strengths.evidence: 00:00-00:15 showed attention moving across more than one part of the room. 00:15-00:30 showed attention moving across more than one part of the room.
top_strengths.what_to_repeat: Reuse the left-center-right room sweep that already looks natural.

top_strengths.title: Upright confident presence
top_strengths.evidence: 00:15-00:30 held an upright, settled stance rather than a collapsed or guarded one.
top_strengths.what_to_repeat: Keep the same upright, settled stance between points and transitions.

priority_actions.title: Tighten the peak size of gestures
priority_actions.why_it_matters: Large bursts of motion can distract from the teaching point when they are not tightly timed.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 had gesture peaks that looked larger than the teaching point required. 00:30-00:45 had gesture peaks that looked larger than the teaching point required.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: Keep big gestures for true emphasis and use smaller controlled hand movements elsewhere.

priority_actions.title: Soften the visible facial tone
priority_actions.why_it_matters: A more relaxed facial tone can make explanations feel warmer and less guarded.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 showed a flatter or tighter visible facial tone than the rest of the clip. 00:15-00:30 showed a flatter or tighter visible facial tone than the rest of the clip.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: Reset the face between sentences and let the expression relax before the next point.

additional_observation_inventory.title: Add more purposeful gesture emphasis
additional_observation_inventory.evidence: 00:15-00:30 stayed physically still through explanation beats that could carry more emphasis.
additional_observation_inventory.suggested_response: Check whether key explanation beats now have one visible gesture cue instead of a static stance.

additional_observation_inventory.title: Turn back toward the audience sooner
additional_observation_inventory.evidence: 00:45-01:00 spent longer than needed turned away from the audience after checks.
additional_observation_inventory.suggested_response: Check whether board or note checks end with a quick shoulder-and-chin reset back to the room.

additional_observation_inventory.title: Open the stance between points
additional_observation_inventory.evidence: 00:45-01:00 showed a more guarded arm-and-shoulder position between teaching points.
additional_observation_inventory.suggested_response: Check whether transitions keep the elbows and shoulders open instead of folding inward.
